schema.appDescription: Calculate the total corpus needed at retirement to sustain your desired lifestyle. Factor in inflation, investment returns, and life expectancy.

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the input fields for the Retirement Corpus Calculator.

howToSteps.name: View instant results
howToSteps.text: Results update in real time as you type.

howToSteps.name: Explore breakdown
howToSteps.text: Open the year-by-year breakdown to see numbers over time.

howToSteps.name: Compare scenarios
howToSteps.text: Adjust inputs to compare scenarios and optimise your decision.

faq.q: How much corpus do I need to retire in India?
faq.a: A popular rule: corpus = 25× annual expenses (4% withdrawal rule). For ₹1 lakh/month expenses at retirement (inflation-adjusted), annual = ₹12 lakh, corpus = ₹3 crore. But with Indian inflation at 6%, medical costs, and increasing longevity, 30× (3.3% withdrawal rate) is safer.

faq.q: How does inflation affect retirement planning?
faq.a: ₹80,000/month today becomes ₹2,57,000/month in 25 years at 6% inflation. This is why you cannot plan retirement based on today's expenses — you must inflation-adjust. The corpus must be large enough to sustain inflating expenses throughout the retirement period.

faq.q: What is the safe withdrawal rate for Indian retirees?
faq.a: The 4% rule (developed for US markets) may be aggressive for India. With 6% inflation and potential market volatility, 3–3.5% withdrawal rate is safer. At 3%: corpus = 33× annual expenses. At 3.5%: corpus = 28× annual expenses. The lower the withdrawal rate, the lower the risk of outliving money.
No. Calculations run in your browser.
Yes. Works on all modern smartphones.

examples.heading: 35-year-old targeting retirement at 60
examples.text: Current monthly expenses ₹80,000. At 6% inflation, expenses at 60 = ₹3,43,000/month. Corpus needed (3% withdrawal rate): ₹3,43,000 × 12 / 0.03 = ₹13.7 crore. Monthly SIP at 12% for 25 years to reach ₹13.7 crore: ₹65,000/month.

examples.heading: Early retirement at 50 — higher corpus needed
examples.text: Same person targeting retirement at 50 (35 years of retirement, more inflation): corpus needed ≈ ₹6.2 crore (expenses lower as retirement is earlier). Monthly SIP at 12% for 15 years: ₹1,45,000/month. Early retirement requires dramatically higher monthly investment.
